title: Boat Rental in Busan: Costs, Marinas and Tips 2026
html:
Boat rental costs in Busan vary by season and boat size, with most private charters leaving from Suyeong Bay Marina near Haeundae. Expect a day price band of roughly EUR 350 to EUR 2,500 in 2026, and BoatHire24 lists verified vessels you can compare before booking.

Busan sits on Korea's south-eastern coast where the warm Korea Strait meets a skyline of bridges and beaches. A charter brings the Gwangan Bridge, the Igidae cliffs and the rocky Oryukdo islets into view from the water. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Busan or list your own.

Where to set off in Busan?

Most Busan charters depart from Suyeong Bay Marina, the largest pleasure harbour in the city and home to the main yacht fleet. From there, captains run across Suyeong Bay toward Haeundae and Gwangalli, where the Gwangan Bridge frames the skyline after dark.

Other landmarks worth naming when you enquire include Haeundae Beach, Gwangalli Beach, the Oryukdo islets off Yeongdo, the Igidae cliffs, and Songjeong further east. The Oryukdo lighthouse lies roughly four nautical miles from the marina.

Which boat to rent in Busan?

Your choice depends on group size, budget and whether you want speed or shade. Busan suits motor boats and crewed yachts more than bareboat sailing, given the busy offshore traffic.

  1. Small motorboat best for couples and quick coastal spins, seating up to 6 guests, from EUR 350 for a half day.
  2. RIB or sportsboat best for a fast run to Oryukdo and back, carrying up to 8 guests, from EUR 450 for a half day.
  3. Sailing yacht best for a relaxed full day in Suyeong Bay, seating up to 10 guests, from EUR 700.
  4. Motor cruiser best for families wanting shade and comfort, carrying up to 12 guests, from EUR 900 for the day.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht best for events and special occasions, hosting up to 12 guests, from EUR 2,000; you can compare the Maiora 26m as an example of this class.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Busan?

Prices move with the calendar, the size of the vessel and whether fuel and a skipper are included, so confirm what the day rate covers before booking. To see live pricing you can browse boats for your dates in a few clicks.

  • A shared group cruise around Gwangalli typically costs from EUR 30 to EUR 60 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half day starts at around EUR 350 in Busan.
  • A catamaran for a full day generally runs from EUR 1,200 to EUR 1,800.
  • A crewed yacht for the day commonly ranges from EUR 2,000 to EUR 2,500 in peak season.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Busan?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm seas and long days make the Korea Strait ideal for cruising. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and quieter, while winter brings the cold, dry north-westerly that locals call the samb-han wind. South Korea generally requires the operator to hold the relevant licence for the vessel, and the operator confirms exact requirements before your trip, so a skippered booking removes any paperwork on your side.

Book a morning slot in July or August: the sea is calmest before noon, and you will reach the Oryukdo islets before the afternoon swell and the summer haze roll in off the strait.

How does booking actually work?

Booking is straightforward once you know your date and group size, and a clear walkthrough of how boat rental works explains deposits, skipper options and what to bring. You message the operator, confirm the boarding point at Suyeong Bay Marina, and pay securely online.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 350 to EUR 2,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 3 to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: up to 12 on most charters
  • Currency: EUR, quoted alongside Korean won locally
  • Main marina: Suyeong Bay Marina, Haeundae district

Questions about boat rental in Busan

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Busan?

South Korea requires the person operating the boat to hold the correct licence, so most visitors book a skippered charter and leave the navigation to the crew. The operator confirms the exact requirements before departure.

How many people can join a charter in Busan?

Most private charters in Busan carry up to 12 guests, while smaller motorboats suit groups of 6 or fewer. Larger crewed yachts can host events, though the limit depends on the vessel and its certification.

How far is it to the Oryukdo islets?

The Oryukdo islets sit roughly four nautical miles from Suyeong Bay Marina, an easy out-and-back trip of two to three hours, leaving time to pause near Igidae for swimming and photographs.

Can I rent a boat for a sunset cruise?

Yes, sunset and evening cruises are popular for the lit-up Gwangan Bridge, and operators run two to three hour trips across Gwangalli Bay. Book ahead in summer, as evening slots fill quickly.

A day afloat is the finest way to understand why Busan grew up around its harbour, from the bridges at dusk to the islets at dawn. Pick your date, check availability, and let a verified local crew handle the rest.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Busan and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
